strengths: Clearly identifies three concrete, real-world objects (JWST, Vinland Map, vintage gym book) with contextual details for each.
Establishes direct links between each object and the IA prompt (âWhy do we seek knowledge?â), showing purposeful alignment with the TOK task.
Uses evidence and citations to support the contributions of the objects, moving beyond mere description to a degree of justification.
weaknesses: Analysis sometimes remains largely descriptive and lacks consistent application of TOK concepts and terminology for deeper insight.
Citation and bibliographic details are incomplete and inconsistently formatted, which undermines the reliability of evidence.
Justifications can be repetitive and occasionally unclear, with uneven precision across the three objects reducing overall coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The strand assessment places this exhibition firmly in the 5â6 band: three objects are clearly identified, links to the prompt are explained, and contributions are justified with some evidence. However, the work exhibits stronger performance than a mid-band submissionâits object selection and contextualization are precise, and its justifications are generally coherent. At the same time, it falls short of the top bands due to uneven analytical depth, descriptive tendencies, and inconsistent referencing. Assigning 8 out of 10 acknowledges the studentâs clear understanding and solid execution while recognizing the need for greater precision, TOK concept application, and rigorous referencing to reach the highest level.

annotations.comment: The student clearly states the IA prompt âWhy do we seek knowledge?â, which establishes focus for the exhibition and aligns directly with the TOK task requirements.
Consider adding a brief overview of the three chosen objects in your introduction to guide the readerâs expectations and show how they collectively address the prompt.
The caption lacks the specific institution or collection where this JWST image is archived, reducing the real-world context of the object.
The paragraph remains largely descriptive. Incorporate specific TOK terminology (e.g., ways of knowing, shared knowledge) to deepen the analysis of why JWST exemplifies the quest for knowledge.

You mention peer deeper into the universe, but the connection to the question âWhy do we seek knowledge?â requires an explicit link explaining how this technological leap illustrates human curiosity driving knowledge-building.
Including a citation for Noel, D. should follow consistent formatting (author, date, title, source) rather than a parenthetical ân.d.â to improve reliability.
Selecting the JWST demonstrates clear real-world context and relevance; it embodies a tangible example of humanityâs drive to expand knowledge through technological innovation.

The second JWST paragraph blends descriptive claims with minimal critical insight. Consider structuring analysis around a TOK concept (e.g., perception, reason) to illustrate how this discovery challenges prior understanding.
This text block includes both JWST analysis and introduction of the Vinland Map, which interrupts focus. Separate these discussions for clarity and stronger argument flow.
The map caption omits the specific collection or archive location, which weakens the real-world context required for this objectâs justification.
Specify the provenance and details of the Vinland Map debate (e.g., which institution conducted radiocarbon tests) to strengthen the authenticity discussion.
This paragraph on the Vinland Map is largely descriptive; it would benefit from deeper analysis of how authenticity inquiries reveal our motives for seeking knowledge.
The claim that loanwords prove cultural exchange requires citation of specific linguistic studies; without this, the evidence remains unsupported.
Incorporate TOK concepts such as evidence and certainty when analysing the Vinland Map to move beyond narrative description.
Clarify which copy of the Vinland Map you examined (e.g., Beinecke Library) and any methods used in authenticity testing to add academic rigor.
The gym book heading should include publication details (author, publisher, date) to ground the object in its real-world provenance.
The personal context of the gym book is generic. Explain how this specific family hand-me-down uniquely illuminates motivations for seeking knowledge.
Forge a clearer connection between the gym bookâs content and the IA prompt by discussing how pursuit of well-being parallels the quest for knowledge.
The claim that the old magazine is unreliable lacks evidence. Provide citations comparing content credibility between vintage and modern fitness sources.
Discuss how the gym book reflects personal versus shared knowledge to deepen the TOK analysis of how individuals pursue understanding.
No reference is provided for the gym magazine. Add a full bibliographic entry to support the objectâs credibility.
Consider adding a concise conclusion that synthesizes how all three objects collectively answer âWhy do we seek knowledge?â for a coherent exhibition close.
The NASA Science reference lacks an access date and should follow consistent academic formatting (e.g., APA or MLA).
The Vinland Map citation is incompleteâmissing publisher, date, and URLâreducing reference reliability.
Citation formatting is inconsistent across entries (italicization, indentation). Apply a uniform style guide for professionalism and clarity.
Ensure all online sources (e.g., NASA FAQ) include retrieval dates alongside URLs to enhance verifiability.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
